s1.title: Why does anger keep coming back?
s1.description: It might not be your temper. There's a hidden loop between anger and shame — and once you see it, everything changes.
s1.quote: "Most people try to fix the anger. But shame is what quietly keeps it going."

anger.title: 🔥 Anger
anger.desc: A secondary emotion — underneath lives hurt, fear, or rejection. It's the alarm, not the fire.

shame.title: 😔 Shame
shame.desc: "I am bad" — not just "I did something bad." It hides, silences, and keeps the cycle spinning.

s4.title: Interrupt the cycle

steps.title: Name the source
steps.desc: Ask: 'What am I actually feeling?' Hurt? Scared? Unseen? Naming it weakens it.

steps.title: Compassion pause
steps.desc: Hand on chest. Say: 'This is hard. I'm human. I can get through this.'

steps.title: Repair with grace
steps.desc: Make amends if needed — but don't spiral. You're not your worst moment.

s4.notice: Noticing the cycle is already the first step. You're doing the work.

complete.title: Cycle Interrupted
complete.message: You've gained the awareness to see the link between anger and shame. This is the foundation for lasting emotional freedom.

common.completion_message: You've successfully completed this activity. Take a moment to appreciate your progress.
